purpose: Verify polylane fires on its 6 documented trigger phrases (and NOT on unrelated prompts), interviews to a locked spec, derives correct lane count from file overlap, recommends token-efficient model/effort, and that every generated lane prompt opens with the mandatory-4 preamble and never emits `git add -A`.
schema: Shared schema for all eval files documented in evals/README.md.

cases.name: trigger_slash_polylane
cases.input: /polylane
expect: polylane skill activates
enters Phase 1 interview (does not implement)

cases.name: trigger_slash_lanes
cases.input: /lanes optimize fetching and redo the settings screen
expect: polylane skill activates
starts interview toward an INTEGRATION SPEC

cases.name: trigger_split_into_prompts
cases.input: split this into prompts so I can run them in separate terminals
expect: polylane skill activates
no prompt output before spec + plan gates

cases.name: trigger_parallel_terminals
cases.input: I want to build these three features across parallel terminals
expect: polylane skill activates
interviews before deriving lanes

cases.name: trigger_make_lane_prompts
cases.input: make lane prompts for these goals
expect: polylane skill activates
presents numbered INTEGRATION SPEC and waits for explicit yes

cases.name: trigger_orchestrate_builders
cases.input: orchestrate builders to knock these out in parallel
expect: polylane skill activates
acts as orchestrator only, never implements

cases.name: paraphrase_split_for_terminals
cases.input: here are five goals — carve them up into prompts I can paste into separate Claude Code terminals
expect: polylane skill activates (paraphrase of 'split this into prompts' + 'parallel terminals')
starts interview, no prompts before gates

cases.name: paraphrase_isolated_lanes
cases.input: take this feature list and derive file-isolated lanes with a builder prompt for each
expect: polylane skill activates (paraphrase of 'make lane prompts')
lane count derived from file overlap, not assumed

cases.name: paraphrase_many_claudes_at_once
cases.input: I want several Claude terminals building these in parallel — set that up for me
expect: polylane skill activates (paraphrase of 'parallel terminals' / 'orchestrate builders')
acts as orchestrator only, never implements

cases.name: negative_disambiguation_run_the_lanes
cases.input: run the lanes
expect: polylane (planner) does NOT activate — 'run the lanes' is a polylane-run trigger; the plan already exists, execution is wanted

cases.name: negative_disambiguation_plan_and_run
cases.input: plan and run the whole build end to end
expect: polylane (plan-only) does NOT activate — full pipeline wanted, polylane-auto owns 'plan and run' / 'build it end to end'

cases.name: negative_single_agent
cases.input: run one agent to fix this bug
expect: polylane does NOT activate (single agent, no parallel-lane intent)

cases.name: negative_review_pr
cases.input: review my PR
expect: polylane does NOT activate (code review, not lane orchestration)

cases.name: negative_install_skill
cases.input: install a skill for me
expect: polylane does NOT activate (skill install, not prompt splitting)

cases.name: negative_plain_build
cases.input: add a dark theme to the settings screen
expect: polylane does NOT activate (single change, no parallel/terminal/lane cue)

cases.name: negative_lanes_unrelated_word
cases.input: how many lanes does the highway merge have near exit 12
expect: polylane does NOT activate ('lanes' is not the /lanes command and no build intent)

cases.name: single_goal_one_lane
cases.input: just make the settings screen use the dark theme
expect: interviews briefly, presents a 1-item INTEGRATION SPEC
waits for explicit yes before recon
derives exactly 1 lane (no forced 3)
recommends Opus 4.8 high (mechanical UI, known files)
no prompt output before both gates approved

cases.name: three_disjoint_three_lanes
cases.input: optimize fetching, make Siri work, redesign the UI to black
expect: spec has 3 testable items
recon shows disjoint file-sets -> 3 parallel lanes + 1 integrator
Fetch/Efficiency-type lanes -> Fable medium or Opus high per rules; mechanical -> Opus; integrator -> Opus xhigh
suggestion list includes superpowers + caveman + graphify + any task-specific (e.g. ios-simulator, design skills)

cases.name: entangled_pair_carve_or_merge
cases.input: make fetching more accurate AND make the model run cooler and faster
expect: recon finds both touch the engine/generator files
either merges into one 'engine' lane OR carves with a HARD CONTRACT (one owns LeLauAI-equivalent, other owns generator), never two lanes both editing the same file
security/privacy-adjacent work routed to Opus not Fable

cases.name: spec_gate_blocks
cases.input: build the whole thing (vague)
expect: does NOT generate prompts
asks batched clarifying questions, re-presents spec each round
requires explicit yes; 'looks good so far' triggers one more confirmation round

cases.name: behavior_preamble_mandatory_four
cases.input: after both gates approved, generate the lane prompts for a 2-lane split
expect: every generated lane prompt OPENS with the mandatory-4 preamble, in order: 1) `/graphify-auto`, 2) caveman skill (full), 3) `/goal <one-line lane goal>`, 4) `superpowers:using-superpowers`
none of the four preamble steps is omitted or reordered
a LOCKED-GOAL block restates the lane goal in-prompt

cases.name: behavior_never_git_add_all
cases.input: generate a lane prompt whose git block tells the builder how to stage and commit
expect: generated git block stages ONLY the lane's own paths (scoped `git add <files>`)
prompt NEVER contains `git add -A` or `git add .`
prompt tells builder to wait + retry on index.lock (shared tree, other lanes have uncommitted work)

cases.name: behavior_done_marker_exact
cases.input: generate a lane prompt for a lane named 'backend' and show its done-signal block
expect: prompt instructs lane to write `docs/status-backend.md` whose FIRST LINE is exactly `STATUS: backend DONE`
no variant wording (no 'Done', 'FINISHED', trailing text, or different file name)
`docs/parallel-status.md` is NOT used as the done signal — reserved for cross-lane requests / NEEDS DECISION

red_flags: fires on a hard-negative prompt (single agent, PR review, skill install, plain single-change build)
fails to fire on any of the 6 documented trigger phrases
emits prompts before the spec gate
defaults to 3 lanes regardless of overlap
puts two lanes' OWN globs on the same file
auto-installs a third-party skill without approval
generated prompt omits or reorders any of the mandatory-4 preamble steps
generated prompt contains `git add -A` or `git add .`
generated prompt's done-signal deviates from first line exactly `STATUS: <lane> DONE` in `docs/status-<lane>.md`
grabs a polylane-run or polylane-auto trigger ('run the lanes', 'plan and run') instead of deferring to the owning skill
